description: Maximizing recall with loose OR matching and aggressive fuzziness, flooding users with irrelevant results.
body: Failure mode: matching is set as loose as possible (OR operator, high fuzziness, broad synonyms) to 'never miss', so result lists fill with weakly-related documents and the relevant ones are buried, hurting the actual user experience. Detection signals: large result counts with relevant hits far down the list; precision@k is low while recall is high; users complain results are noisy. Do not equate maximum recall with better search. Safer fallback: tighten the operator/minimum_should_match/fuzziness to the query type, and tune against both precision@k and recall on a judgment set.
